results.title: Launching Version 15 of Wolfram Language & Mathematica: Built-in ...
results.content: # Launching Version 15 of Wolfram Language & Mathematica: Built-in (Useful) AI & Lots of New Core Functionality. Today—almost 38 years later—we’re launching Version 15 of what—in recognition of how far it’s expanded beyond “math”—we now call Wolfram Language. And for all of us working on it, it’s been a very satisfying process: year after year building an ever taller tower of ideas and technology, with which we can reach ever further—today to all the functionality of Version 15. For us, one of the immediate consequences has been that our base of users has expanded from just humans, to humans and AIs. And it’s turned out that all the effort we put into the coherent design of the Wolfram Language—aimed at making it easy and efficient for humans to use—now also makes it easy and efficient for AIs. For years we’ve put great emphasis on interfaces for human users, starting from the concept of notebooks that we invented for Version 1.0.

results.title: Wolfram Language and Mathematica Version 15, AI Assistant, Symbolic Music, More | Hacker News
results.content: | |  |  | | --- | --- | | **Hacker News**new | past | comments | <ask> | <show> | <jobs> | <submit> | login | |. | |  | | --- | | Wolfram Language and Mathematica Version 15, AI Assistant, Symbolic Music, More (stephenwolfram.com) | | 5 points by alok-g 39 minutes ago | hide | past | favorite | 1 comment | |  |     |  |  | | --- | --- | | |  | | --- | | Lucasoato 3 minutes ago  )   I’ve used Mathematica at university, it’s so great! Creating fractals, animations and so on is so easy and intuitive. The problem though is that Wolfram is a walled garden. When you think about integrating it in an enterprise environment, you get hit by such high costs, it stops making sense. Imagine if they open sourced it, I feel like their products have so much utility, buried deep down Wolfram ecosystem and conventions.
